summary: Implementation is architecturally sound and handles the majority of the spec correctly. Four issues require fixing before approval: one critical (settleOnChain called with wrong second argument), one major (parsePriceToBaseUnits duplicated from x402.ts ŌĆö wrong error type thrown), one major (index.ts exports internal types that spec excludes from public surface), one minor (trivially-vacuous test assertion in fastify.test.ts). Factory state cache RPC-stale coverage gap is also noted.

findings.title: settleOnChain called with authorization.from instead of the verify-recovered address
findings.description: core.ts calls `settleOnChain(payload, payload.payload.authorization.from, {...})`. The second argument is the CLAIMED sender from the wire format, not the cryptographically RECOVERED signer returned by `verifyEip3009Authorization`. settle.ts:286 defines the parameter as `recoveredFrom: 0x${string}` ŌĆö semantically the EIP-712 ecrecover result. In the happy path the two values are equal (verify asserts `recovered == from`), so this is not a runtime crash, but it is semantically wrong: the payer field in X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE is populated from `payload.payload.authorization.from` rather than from the verified `verifyResult.recoveredFrom`. The correct fix is to capture the `recoveredFrom` from `verifyResult` (since `VerifyResult` returns `{ ok: true, recoveredFrom }`) and pass that to settleOnChain and to the response.

findings.dimension: Code Readability & Maintainability (3) + Separation of Concerns (2)
findings.title: parsePriceToBaseUnits duplicates parseUsdPrice from x402.ts ŌĆö throws wrong error type
findings.description: `core.ts` defines its own `parsePriceToBaseUnits` function (lines 581-592) that is an almost-exact copy of `parseUsdPrice` in `x402.ts` (line 69-94). The two diverge in one important way: `core.ts` throws a generic `TypeError` while `x402.ts` throws the typed `InvalidPriceError`. The user-spec AC explicitly states 'negative, zero, scientific, whitespace, >6 decimals ąŠčéą▓ąĄčĆą│ą░čÄčéčüčÅ čü `InvalidPriceError`'. By using a private copy that throws `TypeError`, `core.ts` breaks that AC for any caller who catches `InvalidPriceError`. The fix is to import and call `parseUsdPrice` from `x402.ts` directly; the `InvalidPriceError` class is already exported. Remove `parsePriceToBaseUnits`, `USDC_DECIMALS`, `USDC_SCALE`, and `PRICE_RE` from core.ts.
findings.recommendation: Replace the private `parsePriceToBaseUnits` with `import { parseUsdPrice } from './x402.js'` and call `parseUsdPrice(opts.price)` at the use site. Remove the duplicated constants and function.

findings.title: index.ts exports internal core types that the task spec excludes from the public surface
findings.description: index.ts re-exports `PaywallCoreOptions`, `PaywallRequest`, and `PaywallResult` from `core.ts`. The Task 8 spec states: 'index.ts exports exactly: withPaywall, fastifyPaywall, NETWORKS, SecurityLogger interface, and the public types PaywallConfig, NetworkConfig, PaymentRequirements, PaymentPayload, ExactEvmPayload, OpaqueRelayerKey. Internal helpers (verify, settle, replay-store internals, OpaqueRelayerKey extract symbol, FactoryStateCache) are NOT exported.' `PaywallCoreOptions` is explicitly called out as the 'internal options' type in the task spec (distinguished from the public `PaywallConfig`). `PaywallRequest` and `PaywallResult` are adapter infrastructure, not part of the documented public surface. Exposing them locks the public API to internal implementation shapes. The `index.test.ts` test at line 7 asserts exactly `['NETWORKS','OpaqueRelayerKey','fastifyPaywall','withPaywall']` ŌĆö no internal types.
findings.recommendation: Remove the `export type { ... } from './core.js'` block for `PaywallCoreOptions`, `PaywallRequest`, `PaywallResult`, `SecurityEventCatalog`, and `SecurityEventName`. Keep only `SecurityLogger` if it is needed by integrators for typing their logger argument. Alternatively, move the `SecurityLogger` interface to `types.ts` so it can be exported cleanly without pulling in the core-internal types.

findings.title: sentByPreHandler flag is always false ŌĆö test assertion is trivially vacuous
findings.description: In the test 'sets X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE header on passthrough without calling reply.send' (line 77), `sentByPreHandler` is initialized to `false` and the `onSend` hook at line 88 only ever sets it to `false` again. There is no code path that sets it to `true`, so `expect(sentByPreHandler).toBe(false)` (line 96) is a tautology. The intent appears to be detecting whether `reply.send` was called from the preHandler (which would set a flag to true), but the flag is never set to true. The test therefore does not actually assert the absence of a premature `reply.send` ŌĆö it passes unconditionally.
findings.recommendation: Either remove the `sentByPreHandler` variable and assertion entirely (the other assertions in the same test ŌĆö status 200, correct body, correct header ŌĆö already confirm the passthrough works), or implement the guard correctly: set `sentByPreHandler = true` in the onSend hook when the reply was sent (and set it false in the preHandler path to distinguish). The simplest correct version is to just rely on `res.statusCode === 200` and `res.headers['x-payment-response'] === 'value'` which already proves the route handler ran and the preHandler did not short-circuit.

findings.title: Missing test: factory-state RPC error with non-stale cached value returns last-good value
findings.description: The task spec and tech-spec require: 'RPC error during cache fill returns the last good value if non-stale, else propagates as settlement_failed.reason=rpc_5xx'. The existing test at line 297 only covers the 'no cache at all' path (rpc_5xx surfaced). The complementary test ŌĆö 'cache exists, still within TTL, RPC error during vault lookup returns last good cached entry' ŌĆö is absent. This matters because `readFactoryState` has explicit fallback logic (`if (cached !== undefined) return { entry: cached, servedStale: true }`) but no test validates that the request continues to 200/passthrough when the stale-but-valid cache is used.
findings.recommendation: Add a test that: (1) performs a successful first request to warm the cache, (2) resets readContract to reject, (3) sends a second request within the 5s TTL window, and (4) asserts the response is passthrough (not 402), confirming the last-good cached value was served.

findings.title: core.ts does not use buildErrorResponse from errors.ts ŌĆö Content-Type header inconsistency
findings.description: `errors.ts` exports `buildErrorResponse` that uses `'content-type': 'application/json; charset=utf-8'` (lowercase key, charset included). `core.ts` builds its own inline `build402` function returning `'Content-Type': 'application/json'` (title-case key, no charset). Node http adapter then calls `res.writeHead(status, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...result.headers })` which merges both. This inconsistency means the content-type header format depends on which code path generated the 402. The spec says '`Content-Type: application/json`' which is met by core's version, but the duplication is a maintenance risk. Not blocking, but worth consolidating.
findings.recommendation: Either use `buildErrorResponse` from `errors.ts` as the single source of HTTP response envelope construction, or at minimum ensure `core.ts` and `errors.ts` produce identical header key casing and charset handling. If `core.ts` intentionally diverges (e.g. no charset for brevity), document why.

positive_observations: PublicClient lazy-init with Promise dedup is correctly implemented ŌĆö concurrent first requests share one init (core.ts:195-214).
NonceStore is genuinely module-scope (line 118), not closure-scope inside paywall(), satisfying the cross-adapter singleton requirement.
FactoryStateCache vault address immortality is correctly implemented: once non-zero, the vault address is never re-fetched (readVaultAddress:281).
OpaqueRelayerKey is passed opaque through core.ts ŌĆö no extract symbol imported, no JSON.stringify path.
emit() helper correctly wraps each call in individual try/catch with short-circuit on undefined logger.
SecurityEventCatalog is typed with fixed payload shapes per D18, preventing free-form PII-leaking payloads.
payerHash / developerEoaHash / nonceHash are canonically 10 chars ('0x' + 8 hex) and tested rigorously.
fastify.ts Symbol.for('skip-override') stamp is present and correctly documented ŌĆö the 'why' is non-obvious and warrants the comment.
node-http adapter correctly sets X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE BEFORE invoking the user handler (line 39-41).
Source-level emission ownership test (core.test.ts:563-575) is a creative and effective way to assert the 'core owns all SecurityLogger calls' invariant without relying on runtime behavior alone.
NetworkMismatchError catch in core.ts (line 545-554) correctly emits chain_id_mismatch and does not re-throw in the per-request path.
Verify 2-arg call shape is correct (core.ts:464-471) ŌĆö nonceStore lives inside opts, not as a third argument.
